wt

wt

git worktrees, ergonomically.

Open a repository to see its worktrees. They all live in one predictable place: <repo>.worktrees/, right next to the main checkout.

wt keeps all worktrees in so they're easy to find. Moving them is safe: git updates its bookkeeping for you.

Their directories were deleted outside of wt (e.g. manually, or a tmp dir got cleared). Pruning drops the stale bookkeeping; branches are not affected.

Working…

New worktree

Lock worktree

Locking protects it from wt remove and wt prune (and their git equivalents) until you unlock it. It never affects the branch or its commits.

Remove worktree ?

It has uncommitted changes:

    Remove worktrees?

      Branches are NOT deleted — they stay in the repository and can be checked out again from any worktree.

      Some have uncommitted changes:

      Rename worktree

      wt

      Version

      Command line

      This app and the wt command-line tool share the same core.

      Get started: wt --help

      Shell integration (wt switch / wt cd): wt shell-init

      A wt command-line tool shares this app's core.

      Install via Homebrew: brew install didley/tap/wt-cli

      Or via Go: go install github.com/didley/wt/cmd/wt@latest

      Open source

      wt is free and open source, released under the MIT license.

      Author

      By in Melbourne (Naarm), Australia.
